title: 7 Redeeming Marketable Bonds (Government of Canada Bonds and Treasury Bills) at Maturity
body_text:
7	Redeeming Marketable Bonds (Government of Canada Bonds and Treasury Bills) at Maturity
7.1	Determining the Redemption Value
Marketable bonds issued by the federal government include Government of Canada Bonds and Treasury Bills. The following image shows an example of a Government of Canada Market Issue Bond.
Refer to the following table to determine the redemption value for each bond type.
Bond Type
Bond Redemption
If Coupons on Bond
Government of Canada Marketable Bonds
Old-style Canada Savings Bonds
At Maturity Date on bond
Value = par (face) value
Coupon Bond 芒聙聯 remove all matured Coupons and redeem separately.
See Chapter 8, Redeeming Government of Canada and Canada Savings Bonds Coupons.
Treasury Bills
At Maturity Date on bond
Value = par (face) value
Not applicable
7.2	Processing Redemptions
To redeem a Marketable Bond, proceed as follows.
1.
Remove any matured Coupons and process separately. See Chapter 8, Redeeming Government of Canada and Canada Savings Bonds Coupons.
2.
Determine the redemption value.
3.
Obtain the bondholder's signature on the application for redemption.
4.
If the bond is registered, complete the following fields on the back of the bond:
Date
Name and signature of bondholder (must be the same as the name shown on the front of the bond and the signature must be on the bottom left of the back of the bond)
Credit union Endorsement Stamp
Authorized signature of transferor  (this is only if the bond hasn芒聙聶t matured yet and the bond is being sold)
Important
:	Do not complete the Transfer section on the back of the bond.
5.
Obtain a Power of Attorney from the bondholder. If the bond does not contain a Power of Attorney section, complete the Power of Attorney to Sell and Transfer Securities, Form 3788, and attach it to the bond. Bearer Bonds do not require a Power of Attorney.
If using the Power of Attorney to Sell and Transfer Securities, Form 3788, complete sections 2 and 3 on the form. See Figure 1.
Figure 1: POA for Redeeming Bonds
Important
:	The Registered Owner(s) must sign the Power of Attorney exactly as the bond is registered or it will be returned.
6.
Ensure the bondholder芒聙聶s signature is guaranteed by a credit union signing officer authorized per the list on file with Central 1. See Section 4.4, Guarantee and Over-Guarantee of Signatures.
7.
Unless otherwise instructed, stamp the face of the bond with the credit union's Endorsement Stamp. Ensure the stamp can be clearly read and does not cover any identifying Serial Numbers.
8.
If required, prepare a T600 Ownership Certificate for any interest (e.g., matured Coupons). See Section 4.8, Preparing Tax Statements.
See Section 9.2, Preparing the Deposit for Central 1, for further information.
END
